topic: ECDC SARS-CoV-2 positivity W06-W17 base rate
summary: Historical base rate of 4+ consecutive increases in W06-W17 is 1/4 years (2023 only). When conditioned on declining entry + low positivity (matching 2026), rate is 0/4. Current 2026 positivity is 2.0% and declining.
content:
Detailed analysis of ECDC EU/EEA SARS-CoV-2 positivity data:

W06-W17 historical results:
- 2022: Max 2 consecutive increases (NO) - high positivity, declining from Omicron
- 2023: Max 4 consecutive increases (YES) - W06-W09, entering on rising trend
- 2024: Max 2 consecutive increases (NO) - low positivity, declining
- 2025: Max 2 consecutive increases (NO) - low positivity, declining

2026 context: W05 = 2.0%, steady decline from W01 (3.8)

Key patterns at low positivity (<5%):
- Data is very noisy with mean-reverting sawtooth pattern
- 2025 spring wave onset (W13-W24) only achieved max 2 consecutive increases
- 2024 spring wave was strong but started after W17 (4+ consec run was W17-W26)

Sliding window analysis (12 weeks starting from low + declining + W05-W08):
- 0/4 matching windows had 4+ consecutive increases

Monte Carlo:
- IID model (p=0.33): ~8% chance of 4+ consec in 12 weeks
- Markov model (overall autocorrelation): ~18%
- But these overestimate because they use all-period statistics, not W06-W17 specific
